heading: Disclosure of names of persons on relief
label: 605 Disclosure of names of persons on relief
text: Whoever, for political purposes, furnishes or discloses any list or names of persons receiving compensation, employment or benefits provided for or made possible by any Act of Congress appropriating, or authorizing the appropriation of funds for work relief or relief purposes, to a political candidate, committee, campaign manager, or to any person for delivery to a political candidate, committee, or campaign manager; andWhoever receives any such list or names for political purposes—Shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 61e, 61g (, 11:50 a.m., E.S.T., ch. 410, §§ 6, 8, ).Aug. 2, 193953 Stat. 1148This section consolidates sections 61e and 61g of title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed.Reference to persons aiding or assisting, contained in words “or to aid or assist in furnishing or disclosing” was omitted as unnecessary as such persons are made principals by .section 2 of this titleChanges were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $1,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 61e, 61g (, 11:50 a.m., E.S.T., ch. 410, §§ 6, 8, ).Aug. 2, 193953 Stat. 1148This section consolidates sections 61e and 61g of title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed.Reference to persons aiding or assisting, contained in words “or to aid or assist in furnishing or disclosing” was omitted as unnecessary as such persons are made principals by .section 2 of this titleChanges were made in phraseology.
